title: Diphasiastrum thyoides
taxonomy: Diphasiastrum thyoides is a species of club moss within the family Lycopodiaceae. It belongs to the class Lycopodiopsida, and its kingdom is Plantae. Diphasiastrum thyoides is also classified under the phylum Lycopodiophyta. At the rank of division, it falls under Lycopodiophyta as well, with the kingdom still being Plantae. Further classification also includes the rank of order Lycopodiales, and the family Lycopodiaceae.
uses: Diphasiastrum thyoides, also known as the eastern forked polypody, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. intro: Diphasiastrum thyoides, also known as the northern forked polypody, is a type of epiphytic fern native to boreal forests in North America and Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues, wounds, and skin irritations.
